title: General AI Literacy
audience: Anyone who wants a solid practical understanding of AI at work.

questions.text: What is the clearest definition of AI in a workplace context?
options: A system that can perform tasks requiring patterns, prediction, or language reasoning.
Any software that runs in the cloud.
A robot that replaces all human roles.

questions.text: What does a large language model (LLM) primarily do?
options: Predicts likely next tokens to generate text.

questions.text: What is a hallucination in AI output?
options: An intentional lie by the AI.
A confident-sounding but incorrect or unsupported response.
A slow response caused by bad Wi-Fi.

questions.text: Which prompt usually improves response quality most?
options: You know what I mean, just do it.
You are a project analyst. Summarise this in 5 bullet points for execs, include risks and actions.

questions.text: What is the best first step before using AI output in a real decision?
options: Check key facts, assumptions, and sources.

questions.text: Which is safest to avoid sharing in public AI tools unless approved?
What does 'human-in-the-loop' mean?
options: A human writes every line manually.
A human reviews or approves outputs at key control points.

questions.text: Which use case is usually low risk and high value to start with?
options: Drafting summaries, emails, and first-pass documents.

questions.text: What is a practical way to reduce hallucinations?
options: Ask for structured output and require citations/evidence where possible.

questions.text: Why is prompt context important?
options: It makes the AI run on cheaper hardware.
It tells the model audience, intent, and constraints.
It permanently trains the model by default.
It converts output into JSON automatically.

questions.text: What is a sign an AI workflow needs governance?
options: It is used for high-impact outcomes (financial, legal, customer, safety).

questions.text: Which metric matters most for business AI adoption?
options: Number of prompts sent per day.
How often people mention AI in meetings.

questions.text: What is retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) designed to do?
options: Retrieve relevant source content at runtime before generating an answer.

questions.text: In practical terms, what is model drift?
options: When the model file is moved to another folder.
When performance degrades over time as data/context changes.
When users change their writing style.

questions.text: Which is the best way to use AI for drafting comms?
options: Send first draft directly to customers.
Use AI for draft + human edit for tone, facts, and approvals.
Use AI only for subject lines.
Avoid any review to keep speed high.

questions.text: What should you do when AI confidence seems high but evidence is weak?
options: Request verification steps, sources, or independent checks.

questions.text: Which statement about AI and jobs is most accurate?
options: AI will instantly replace every knowledge worker.
AI changes task composition, often augmenting roles and shifting skill needs.
AI has no impact on workflows.

questions.text: What is an effective way to build AI capability in teams?
options: Practical use-case playbooks, training, and measurable pilots.

questions.text: When should an AI output be treated as high risk?
options: When it affects policy, legal exposure, money, safety, or external commitments.
When it is shorter than 100 words.
When it references a meeting note.

questions.text: What is a mature AI operating model aiming for?
options: Centralized approvals for every single prompt.
Balanced speed, quality, safety, and accountability with clear ownership.
